sections.title: History and Heritage
sections.content: The Hertogen of Brabant of the Dukes of Brabant were first a hunting lodge. Then they we're a prison. The castle was built in the 12th century and is one of the oldest castles in Turnhout. It has a rich history, having been used for various purposes over the centuries, including as a residence for nobility, a military fortress, and a prison. The castle's architecture reflects its long history, with elements from different periods visible throughout the structure.

sections.title: The Golden Age
sections.content: During the 14th and 15th centuries, Brabant reached its peak of power and prosperity. The dukes patronized the arts, commissioned magnificent buildings, and established trade networks that connected the region to all corners of Europe. Grand cathedrals, noble residences, and civic buildings rose across the landscape. The court of the dukes became renowned for its sophistication and cultural refinement, rivaling the greatest courts of Western Europe in artistic achievement and political influence.
